description:
# [linear] fix(ci): git-workflow-service fails to push `.github/workflows/` files — HTTPS token missing `workfl

## Situation
This feature was requested but PRD generation encountered an issue.

## Problem
fix(ci): git-workflow-service fails to push `.github/workflows/` files — HTTPS token missing `workflow` scope

## Problem

`git-workflow-service.ts` pushes agent work via HTTPS using the server's OAuth token. GitHub refuses any push that creates or modifies `.github/workflows/*.yml` without the `workflow` OAuth scope. The server token does NOT have this scope.

Any feature whose implementation includes a new or modified GitHub Actions workflow file will hit this failure:

```
[remote rejected] (refusing to allow an OAuth App to create or update workflow
`.github/workflows/<name>.yml` without `workflow` scope)
```

The feature gets blocked with `statusChangeReason: "git workflow failed — uncommitted work in worktree"` and `failureCount: 1`. Recovery requires manual SSH push + PR creation.

## Root Cause

`apps/server/src/services/git-workflow-service.ts` configures the git remote as:

```
https://<token>@github.com/proto-labs-ai/protoMaker.git
```

The token comes from `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var or the server credential store — neither has the `workflow` scope.

## Fix Options

**Option A (preferred)**: Switch `git-workflow-service.ts` to use SSH remote URLs (`git@github.com:proto-labs-ai/protoMaker.git`) for push operations. SSH identity-based auth bypasses OAuth scope requirements entirely.

**Option B**: Ensure the credential token used for git pushes has `workflow` scope. This requires a classic PAT (fine-grained PATs don't support `workflow` scope). Store as `GH_PAT` and use it specifically for workflow-file pushes.

**Option C**: Detect `.github/workflows/` changes in the git-workflow-service and fall back to SSH or a PAT automatically.

## Impact

* Any CI feature (new workflows, workflow edits) will fail on first push
* Agent marks work as blocked with misleading "uncommitted work" error
* Manual recovery required every time

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Agent can commit and push a new `.github/workflows/*.yml` file without manual intervention
- [ ] No change to the existing dev/staging/main branch protection behavior
- [ ] Server logs clearly indicate which push strategy was used (HTTPS vs SSH)

## Approach
To be determined after manual review.

## Results
- Feature is implemented as described
- Tests pass

## Constraints
- Needs manual review and refinement

## Research Notes
Now let me dig into the specific push implementation details and the exact code paths:Now I have all the information needed. Here's the complete structured research summary:

---

## Research Summary: fix(ci) — git-workflow-service workflow scope failure

### Problem Confirmed

The issue is exactly as described. The `git push` calls in three locations all rely on whatever credential mechanism `git` resolves from the environment. The `GH_TOKEN` in `.env` starts with `gho_` (confirmed in `.env` line 36) — this is an **OAuth App token**, which GitHub explicitly blocks from pushing `.github/workflows/*.yml` changes without the `workflow` OAuth scope. No code currently intercepts or handles this failure specially.

---

### Files That Need Modification

| File | Purpose | Change Required |
|------|---------|----------------|
| `apps/server/src/services/git-workflow-service.ts` | Primary push path (auto-mode completion) | Core push logic changes here |
| `apps/server/src/services/worktree-re
